is a historic [Roman Catholic](/wiki/Roman_Catholic "Roman Catholic") church near [Bendena, Kansas](/wiki/Bendena,_Kansas "Bendena, Kansas"). It was built in 1903. The church was listed on the [National Register of Historic Places](/wiki/National_Register_of_Historic_Places "National Register of Historic Places") in 1998.[[1]](#cite_note-nris-1)

Marie Louise Krenner (1998). ["National Register of Historic Places Registration: St. Benedict's Church / 043-0000-200"](https://npgallery.nps.gov/NRHP/GetAsset/NRHP/98000324_text). [National Park Service](/wiki/National_Park_Service "National Park Service"). Retrieved November 20, 2017. From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Historic church in Kansas, United States

**St. Benedict's Church** is a historic [Roman Catholic](/wiki/Roman_Catholic "Roman Catholic") church near [Bendena, Kansas](/wiki/Bendena,_Kansas "Bendena, Kansas"). It was built in 1903. The church was listed on the [National Register of Historic Places](/wiki/National_Register_of_Historic_Places "National Register of Historic Places") in 1998.[[1]](#cite_note-nris-1)

It is a gable-fronted red brick building on a limestone block foundation, which was the foundation of the 1895 church which this replaced. It is 90 by 51 feet (27 m × 16 m) in plan and has a 101 feet (31 m) tower.[[2]](#cite_note-nrhpdoc-2)
